summary: Converted the Melee build workflow into a reusable workflow invoked by the GitHub Pages publishing workflow, so page generation waits for the build before fetching progress data. The PR also narrowed deployment concurrency to the deploy job, removed an explicit race-condition comment from the page-generation entrypoint, added a SvelteKit prepare script for the progress site, and changed progress percentage formatting from floor-based truncation to rounding.
what_changed: Changed `.github/workflows/build-melee.yml` from direct `push` and `pull_request` triggers to `on: workflow_call`, making it reusable from another workflow rather than independently triggered.
In `.github/workflows/publish-pages.yml`, added a `build-melee` job that uses `./.github/workflows/build-melee.yml` and made `gen-pages` depend on it via `needs: build-melee`.
Renamed the pages workflow run name from `Publish GitHub pages` to `Build Melee & publish GitHub Pages`, reflecting the combined build-and-publish sequence.
Moved concurrency control from the entire `publish-pages` workflow to the `deploy-pages` job with `group: deploy` and `cancel-in-progress: false`.
Changed the `build-ninja` job condition in `build-melee.yml` from `github.repository == 'doldecomp/melee'` to `github.actor == 'doldecomp'`.

Removed the comment in `.github/packages/gen-pages/entrypoint.sh` that noted a race condition between `build-melee` and `gen-pages`, while keeping the progress JSON fetch from `https://progress.decomp.club/data/melee/GALE01?format=json&mode=all`.
smart_moves: Resolved the apparent race by expressing the build-to-page-generation ordering in GitHub Actions dependencies rather than relying on timing between separately triggered workflows.
Factored `build-melee` as a reusable `workflow_call`, which lets the Pages workflow invoke the exact build workflow and wait for completion.
Placed concurrency on the deploy job instead of the whole publish workflow, preserving serialized deployments without unnecessarily blocking or conflating the build and page-generation stages.
Removed the stale race-condition comment after the workflow dependency was introduced, avoiding misleading documentation in the page-generation entrypoint.
decomp_lessons: For progress dashboards that consume generated build/progress artifacts, encode producer-consumer ordering directly with workflow job dependencies such as `needs`, not with separate workflows that can race.
Reusable GitHub Actions workflows are useful for decomp CI because the same build definition can be run directly by dependent workflows without duplicating build logic.
Small UI fixes in progress reporting, such as rounding instead of flooring percentages, can improve reported progress accuracy and presentation without changing underlying matching data.
Keep CI comments synchronized with workflow behavior; once a race has been structurally fixed, remove or update comments that describe the old failure mode.
naming_conventions: Workflow and job names stayed descriptive: `build-melee`, `publish-pages`, `Build Melee`, `Generate pages`, and `Deploy to GitHub Pages`.
The pages workflow run name was updated to `Build Melee & publish GitHub Pages` to make the combined CI purpose visible in GitHub Actions.
The progress endpoint continues to use the game/version identifier `GALE01` in the URL.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify `.github/workflows/build-melee.yml` after it became a reusable `workflow_call` workflow.
Search for other GitHub Actions workflows that fetch `progress.decomp.club` data and may need explicit `needs` dependencies.
Check whether the `github.actor == 'doldecomp'` condition was retained or revised in subsequent CI changes.
Look for later progress-site changes to `formatPercent` or trophy/progress display behavior.
